title: rubus occidentalis subsp. leucodermis
intro: Rubus occidentalis subsp. leucodermis, also known as the Western Thimbleberry, is a subspecies of the black raspberry plant native to North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to its high content of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Western Thimbleberry are numerous and include reducing pain and inflammation, improving digestive health, and supporting the immune system. Western Thimbleberry can be prepared into various herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its benefits.
